text: I reached out and stopped Lucia, lowering my voice. “Don’t move. I’ll hold them off—you find a way to get close to the mentor.”
Her eyes widened, lips turning pale. “Are you insane?!”
I took a deep breath and stepped out of the shadows. Blue light reflected from the ground onto my face as I raised my empty hand.
Lucia retreated into the darkness, leaving only the leader and me facing each other.
“Don’t misunderstand,” I tried to steady my voice. “We’re not enemies. I just want to confirm—whether she’s still alive.”
The black-robed leader slowly turned his head, his voice like stone grinding against iron. “Alive or dead is irrelevant to fate. Mortal, do not test the order of the Echo.”
🎲【Charisma Check: Failed】Your voice trembles slightly, and he detects a hint of hesitation.
“Stalling?” he sneers. “You can’t even lie properly.”
Blue light suddenly gathers, the runes of the ritual pulsing like a heartbeat.
My heart tightens. The air begins to warp, heat surging outward.
A whip of light lashes out and strikes my chest.
Blazing pain spreads from my chest. I’m thrown to the ground, the taste of blood rising in my throat.
Lucia bursts from the shadows, her staff flaring brightly as she shields me behind her.
“Stop!” she shouts, her incantation nearly drowning out the roar.
A golden wall of light unfolds before us, blocking the next strike.
But the ritual’s glow begins to spiral out of control. Blue flames crawl along the floor, threatening to consume the entire chamber.
The leader’s laughter echoes through the blazing light. “The cycle must be completed! Even if flesh itself must be the offering!”
He raises both hands, the runes blazing brighter.
“In your dreams!” Lucia cries out.
Her spell tears through the air, the light barrier expanding and separating me from Alicia.
“Lucia! Unlock it!” I roar.
Her staff erupts with dazzling light, the runes along the chains beginning to fracture.
The leader senses the disruption—but the ritual is already unstable. Backlash energy surges like a tidal wave.
Blue light explodes outward, the shockwave overturning everything.
When I force my eyes open, the robed figures are gone. Alicia and Lucia lie unconscious beside the shattered stone.
“…Thank you…” she murmurs faintly in her unconscious state.
Wind pours in through the collapsed entrance, snowflakes settling on her face as if to cover everything.
I let out a bitter smile. Fate has never been fair.
